description: Fifth curated wave promoting stable client-only rules from the legacy main-project blocklist and repeated runtime review cases.

rules.reason: Legacy blocklist and repeated HUD-side corpus usage consistently classify AppleSkin as client-only.
rules.notes: Also resolves the remaining Fabric review case that was still oscillating between shared metadata and UI-only evidence.

rules.reason: Legacy blocklist and repeated review hits only expose client-side ambience assets and audio hooks.
rules.notes: Promoted to remove repeated sound-only review cases from Forge and NeoForge packs.

rules.reason: Legacy blocklist and runtime review history both indicate a menu-only client mod with no server-side evidence.
rules.notes: Includes file-name fallback so malformed Fabric jars without parsed metadata still match the curated rule.

rules.reason: Legacy blocklist and review evidence consistently point to a purely client-side advancement UI helper.
rules.notes: Promoted after repeated runtime review cases in HUD-oriented packs.

rules.reason: Legacy blocklist identifies Controlling as a keybind-search UI mod with client-only behavior.
rules.notes: Fabric is already covered by an earlier curated wave; this extends the same decision to Forge and NeoForge.

rules.reason: Legacy blocklist and the existing main-project test suite both treat Embeddium as a client graphics optimization mod.
rules.notes: Promoted with higher confidence because the main project already relies on this classification in automated tests.

rules.reason: Legacy blocklist and corpus metadata consistently mark Euphoria Patcher as a client shader-side helper.
rules.notes: Includes file-name fallback to cover malformed Fabric jars that fail metadata extraction but still appear in review.

rules.reason: Legacy blocklist and corpus prevalence both identify FancyMenu as a client menu customization mod.
rules.notes: Promoted to suppress high-volume client UI false-positive review cases across all supported loaders.

rules.reason: Legacy blocklist and dependency patterns link Konkrete to client UI stacks such as FancyMenu.
rules.notes: Used as the shared client-side library behind multiple menu customization mods.

rules.reason: Legacy blocklist consistently treats Melody as a client audio library used by menu customization stacks.
rules.notes: Promoted together with FancyMenu and Konkrete to keep the whole client-only menu stack aligned.

rules.reason: Legacy blocklist classifies Mouse Tweaks as a client input helper with no server-side execution path.
rules.notes: Fabric is already covered by an earlier curated wave; this fills the Forge and NeoForge gap.

rules.reason: Legacy blocklist and renderer-only shader integration history both classify Oculus as client-only.
rules.notes: Promoted with higher confidence because it is the Forge-side shader companion to Iris.

rules.reason: Legacy blocklist and repeated runtime review hits consistently point to a client-side chunk rendering optimization mod.
rules.notes: Promoted specifically to remove remaining review noise from graphics optimization packs.
